name: Potato Rolls
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Southern Living
Preparation Time: 1 hour
1. Cook potatoes in boiling water to cover for 15 minutes, or until tender. Drain and reserve 1 cup of the water, then set aside.
2. Cool reserved water to between 105°F and 115°F. Stir yeast and sugar into the cool water; let stand for 5 minutes.
3. Mash enough potatoes to measure 1 cup and add them to a large mixing bowl. Add melted butter, shortening, honey, beaten eggs, salt, yeast mixture, and 2-1/2 cups of flour. Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed for 2 minutes.
4. Gradually stir in the remaining flour until a soft dough forms.
5. Turn dough onto a floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic (about 8 to 10 minutes).
6. Place in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free from drafts, for 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
7. Punch dough down and divide into thirds. Shape each third into balls.
8. Place shaped rolls in three greased 9-inch round cake pans. Let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, for 40 to 50 minutes, or until doubled in bulk.
9. Bake at 400°F for 20 to 25 minutes. Yield: 45 rolls.
NOTE: Potato Rolls may be wrapped in aluminum foil, then placed in a freezer bag and frozen up to 2 months. To reheat, let thaw almost to room temperature, place in cake pans, cover with foil, and bake at 400°F for 10 minutes or until thoroughly heated.
